chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, the topic is Huffman coding.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Huffman coding is a compression technique.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is used for reducing the size of data or message.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose you want to store the data in a file so you can store the data in compressed form

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: to reduce the size of a file.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: When the data is sent over a network, data can be compressed and sent to reduce the cost

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: of transmission.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In this video, I am going to explain you if a normal message is sent what will be the

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: cost and what is the fixed size encoding then what is variable size encoding that is Huffman

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: coding.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us study the problem.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have a message here.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now this message I want to store it in a file or I want to send it over a network.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then let us understand what will be the cost of a message.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the cost of this message or the size of the message will be measured in terms of bits.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us check.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First of all there are 20 letters in this one.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the length of this message is 20.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next how this message has to be sent.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It has to be sent by using ASCII code.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Modern devices or computers all use ASCII codes for characters or English alphabets.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So these are all characters.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: ASCII codes are 8 bits.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have used alphabets A to E here so that is easy to understand for us.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if I say A its ASCII code is 65 and the binary form is this one.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So total 8 bits.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: These are 8 bits that are representing decimal 65 and that is representing A. Similarly if

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I say B then it is 66.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will be for 66.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Likewise C, D, E for everyone we have that is 667 and 68 and 69 and here the binary code

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: will be there.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 8 bits are there for each alphabet.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now if normally this message is sent 8 bit for each alphabet then total number of bits

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: will be how much as there are 20 alphabets in this message.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will be 8 into 20 that is 160 bits.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Size of the message will be 160 bits.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now this is the simple message sent without encoding.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The size will be 160 bits.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 8 bit for each alphabet.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us look at can we use our own codes instead of ASCII codes.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How it is possible?

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See I am not using all English alphabets or all possible symbols on the keyboard.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I am not using every symbol from the character set.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I am using only 5 alphabets that took capital letters.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then do I need 8 bit codes for representing just 5 alphabets?

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Few bits are sufficient.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So can I use my own codes with less amount of bits?

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So in this class we will see fixed size codes.

chunks.text: Here I have taken alphabets in this message and how many times each alphabet is appearing

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have written the count here or you can also call it as frequency.

chunks.text: A is appearing 3 times 1 2 3 likewise you can check all how many times they are appearing

chunks.text: I have written them here.

chunks.text: Total there are 20 alphabets.

chunks.text: If you want to see the frequency then you can say this is 3 by 20 that is 3 by 20 or

chunks.text: this is 5 by 20 because total number are 20 so this is 6 by 20 and you can take the

chunks.text: I prefer taking counts that is easy for working.

chunks.text: What should be the code?

chunks.text: See I have 5 characters.

chunks.text: I don't need 8 bits for just 5 characters.

chunks.text: 8 bit ASCII codes are for 128 characters or 128 symbols but for only 5.

chunks.text: Let us see.

chunks.text: If I have just 1 bit then either I can write 0 or 1.

chunks.text: So I will take 5 out of that.

chunks.text: So let us say in 3 bits I will say A is 0 0 0 B is 0 0 1 C is 0 1 0 D is 0 1 1 and E

Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari

chunks.text: I will use this for encoding this message and this will reduce the size of the message.

chunks.text: So it means here instead of writing ASCII code I will be writing 0 0 1 for B and 0 1

chunks.text: So this is one method with fixed size codes.

chunks.text: Now we will go to variable size code and let us follow Huffman coding which will still

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari

chunks.text: alphabets some characters or alphabets may be appearing few times less number of times

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: entire message will be definitely reduced.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us see what is his idea already we have this table all the alphabets and how

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: many times they are appearing in a message so that gives the frequency of those alphabets.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now Huffman code follows optimal merge pattern.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we have to generate our own code so what should be the method for the code.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So Huffman has given an approach for getting our own variable size codes.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So he says that all those alphabets should be arranged in the increasing order of their

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: count.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the least one and this is the next one and next one and so on.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So they are arranged in the increasing order of their count or frequencies.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the first the smallest one then the highest one.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this optimal merge pattern should be followed on this one.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what I should do merge two smallest one and make one node.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 2 plus 3 this is 5.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now next select two smallest and form a node.

chunks.title: Huffman Coding - Greedy Method - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 5 4 5 6 these are remaining out of this which are two smaller either I can take this

chunks.text: 5 4 or that 5 4 so I will take this one.

chunks.text: So I will combine this one with this.

chunks.text: Now what are the list I have or the nodes I have 9 5 6 these are the two minimum.

chunks.text: So this gives what 20.

chunks.text: So here we got a optimal merge pattern tree.

chunks.text: So actually follow optimal merge pattern that is greedy approach.

text: Hi, the topic is Huffman coding. Huffman coding is a compression technique. It is used for reducing the size of data or message. Suppose you want to store the data in a file so you can store the data in compressed form to reduce the size of a file. When the data is sent over a network, data can be compressed and sent to reduce the cost of transmission. In this video, I am going to explain you if a normal message is sent what will be the cost and what is the fixed size encoding then what is variable size encoding that is Huffman coding. Now let us study the problem. I have a message here. Now this message I want to store it in a file or I want to send it over a network. Then let us understand what will be the cost of a message. So the cost of this message or the size of the message will be measured in terms of bits. Let us check. First of all there are 20 letters in this one. So the length of this message is 20. Next how this message has to be sent. It has to be sent by using ASCII code. Modern devices or computers all use ASCII codes for characters or English alphabets. So these are all characters. ASCII codes are 8 bits. I have used alphabets A to E here so that is easy to understand for us. So if I say A its ASCII code is 65 and the binary form is this one. So total 8 bits. These are 8 bits that are representing decimal 65 and that is representing A. Similarly if I say B then it is 66. This will be for 66. Likewise C, D, E for everyone we have that is 667 and 68 and 69 and here the binary code will be there. So 8 bits are there for each alphabet. Now if normally this message is sent 8 bit for each alphabet then total number of bits will be how much as there are 20 alphabets in this message. So this will be 8 into 20 that is 160 bits. Size of the message will be 160 bits. Now this is the simple message sent without encoding. The size will be 160 bits. 8 bit for each alphabet.
